propositions: Attestations are currently signed with a symmetric HMAC keyed on a per-environment secret, which two upcoming epics both need to replace with asymmetric verify-by-anyone signatures.
A single shared attestation substrate provides sign/verify over DSSE PAE bytes so verification never re-serializes parsed JSON and never trusts an in-band algorithm.
A pluggable scheme registry with a per-kind policy table selects scheme, namespace, and trust-root out-of-band rather than from record data, closing alg-confusion attacks.

An SSHSIG scheme uses ssh-keygen -Y via subprocess with no new core dependency, verifying offline and failing closed on tampered bytes, wrong namespace, unknown principal, substituted key, or expired window.
The existing HMAC signer is registered as a legacy scheme so prior plan-review and completion-verifier attestations continue to verify without regression.
An ADR documents the DSSE envelope, pluggable scheme, per-kind policy table, domain separation, and HMAC-as-legacy design.
